import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut token = ""
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
        if string.len(token) > 0
          match string.parse_int(token)
          when Some(v)
            result = list.push(result, v)
          when None
            ()
          end
          token = ""
        end
      else
        token = "#{token}#{string.from_byte(b)}"
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if string.len(token) > 0
    match string.parse_int(token)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let nums = parse_ints(line1)
let target = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
when Some(v)
  v
when None
  0
end

let n = list.len(nums)
mut found = false
mut fi = 0
mut fj = 0
mut i = 0
while i < n and not found
  mut j = i + 1
  while j < n and not found
    let a = match list.get(nums, i)
    when Some(x)
      x
    when None
      0
    end
    let b = match list.get(nums, j)
    when Some(x)
      x
    when None
      0
    end
    if a + b == target
      found = true
      fi = i
      fj = j
    end
    j = j + 1
  end
  i = i + 1
end

if found
  println("found=#{fi},#{fj}")
else
  println("none")
end
